title: Revenue Quality Analysis

content.statement: A dollar of revenue is not equal to every other dollar of revenue.
content.question: What percentage of your business's projected revenue is recurring, high-margin, diversified, and cash-reliable?
content.equation: RQA = Recurring × Margin × Retention × Diversification × Cash Reliability
sop: Calculate what percentage of revenue is Recurring, repeat or subscription-based rather than one-off (see calculation, page 6).
Calculate your average Margin percentage across that revenue (see calculation, page 6).
Calculate Retention, the percentage of that revenue base still with you a year later (see calculation, page 6).
Calculate Diversification and Cash Reliability as percentages, how spread out your revenue sources are and how predictably cash actually lands (see calculation, page 6).
Multiply all five percentages together to see your true Revenue Quality score, and set one target to raise your weakest input next quarter.
content.explainer: Chasing any revenue is beginner; chasing high-quality revenue is operator.
questions: What percentage of your business's projected revenue is recurring, high-margin, diversified, and cash-reliable?
If you had to lose one customer segment or product line tomorrow, would your revenue mix get more fragile or more resilient?
Is your best month of revenue this year the kind you could count on repeating, or a one-time spike you're already treating as normal?

definitions.def: The percentage of customers who keep paying or using a product over time.

content.equation_example: Recurring 70%, Margin 60%, Retention 90%, Diversification 50%, Cash Reliability 80%. 0.70 times 0.60 times 0.90 times 0.50 times 0.80 equals 15.1% out of 100%. Higher percentage means your revenue is durable, diversified, and hard to disrupt. Lower percentage means it looks big on paper but is fragile in more than one way at once.
content.closing_statement: Revenue quality matters more than revenue size.

caption:
Principle Nº 57: Revenue Quality Analysis

A dollar of revenue is not equal to every other dollar of revenue. Chasing any revenue is beginner; chasing high-quality revenue is operator.

Ask yourself: What percentage of your business's projected revenue is recurring, high-margin, diversified, and cash-reliable?
